pokemon:starmie.name: Starmie
pokemon:starmie.summary: Starmie swims through water by spinning its star-shaped body as if it were a propeller on a ship. The core at the center of this Pokémon’s body glows in seven colors.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Starmie swims through water by spinning its star-shaped body as if it were a propeller on a ship. The core at the center of this Pokémon’s body glows in seven colors.
At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.
Its core shines in many colors and sends radio signals into space to communicate with something.
Its central core glows with the seven colors of the rainbow. Some people value the core as a gem.
It is said that it uses the seven- colored core of its body to send electric waves into outer space.
At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.
People in ancient times imagined that STARMIE were transformed from the reflections of stars that twinkled on gentle waves at night.
This POKéMON has a geometric body. Because of its body, the locals suspect that it is an alien creature.
The center section of its body is called the core. It glows in a dif­ ferent color each time it is seen.
The middle section of its body is called the core. It glows in a different color each time it is seen.
Its central core glows with the seven colors of the rainbow. Some people value the core as a gem.
The center section is named the core. People think it is communicating when it glows in seven colors.
The center section is named the core. People think it is communicating when it glows in seven colors.
Its unusual body shape, reminiscent of abstract art, led local people to spread rumors that this Pokémon may be an invader from outer space.
Starmie’s center section—the core—glows brightly in seven colors. Because of its luminous nature, this Pokémon has been given the nickname “the gem of the sea.”
At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.
At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.
Its central core glows with the seven colors of the rainbow. Some people value the core as a gem.
STARMIE’s center section - the core - glows brightly in seven colors. Because of its luminous nature, this POKéMON has been given the nickname “the gem of the sea.”
STARMIE swims through water by spinning its star-shaped body as if it were a propeller on a ship. The core at the center of this POKéMON’s body glows in seven colors.
Starmie swims by spinning its body at high speed. As this Pokémon cruises through the ocean, it absorbs tiny plankton.
Regardless of the environment it lives in, its body grows to form a symmetrical geo­ metric shape.
Regardless of the environment it lives in, its body grows to form a symmetrical geometric shape.
Its shining core is thought to receive and transmit enigmatic signals. It has been known to cause headaches in those who approach it.
This Pokémon has an organ known as its core. The organ glows in seven colors when Starmie is unleashing its potent psychic powers.
It rotates its geometrically shaped body to swim through the water. It always seems to be sending out mysterious radio waves.
Its sparkling core is called “the gem of the sea.” This core can be made into high-priced accessories that are traded in secret.
At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.
Its core shines in many colors and sends radio signals into space to communicate with something.
Its central core glows with the seven colors of the rainbow. Some people value the core as a gem.
At the center of its body is a red core, which sends mysterious radio signals into the night sky.
The center section is named the core. People think it is communicating when it glows in 7 colors.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Starmie swims through water by spinning its star-shaped body as if it were a propeller on a ship. The core at the center of this Pokémon’s body glows in seven colors.

pokemon:staryu.name: Staryu
pokemon:staryu.summary: Staryu apparently communicates with the stars in the night sky by flashing the red core at the center of its body. If parts of its body are torn, this Pokémon simply regenerates the missing pieces and limbs.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Staryu apparently communicates with the stars in the night sky by flashing the red core at the center of its body. If parts of its body are torn, this Pokémon simply regenerates the missing pieces and limbs.
If its body is torn, it can grow back if the red core remains. The core flashes at midnight.
As long as its red core remains, it can regenerate its body instantly, even if it’s torn apart.
An enigmatic POKéMON that can effortlessly regenerate any appendage it loses in battle.
When the stars twinkle at night, it floats up from the sea floor, and its body's center core flickers.
If its body is torn, it can grow back if the red core remains. The core flashes at midnight.
It gathers with others in the night and makes its red core glow on and off with the twinkling stars. It can regenerate limbs if they are severed from its body.
It appears in large numbers by seashores. At night, its central core flashes with a red light.
At night, the cen­ ter of its body slowly flickers with the same rhythm as a human heartbeat.
At night, the middle of its body slowly flickers with the same rhythm as a human heartbeat.
An enigmatic POKéMON that can effortlessly regenerate any appendage it loses in battle.
As long as the center section is unharmed, this Pokémon can grow back fully even if it is chopped to bits.
As long as the center section is unharmed, this Pokémon can grow back fully even if it is chopped to bits.
This Pokémon gets nibbled on by Lumineon and others. Thanks to its red core, it regenerates fast, so it’s unconcerned by their snack attacks.
Staryu’s center section has an organ called the core that shines bright red. If you go to a beach toward the end of summer, the glowing cores of these Pokémon look like the stars in the sky.
If its body is torn, it can grow back if the red core remains. The core flashes at midnight.
If its body is torn, it can grow back if the red core remains. The core flashes at midnight.
An enigmatic POKéMON that can effortlessly regenerate any appendage it loses in battle.
STARYU’s center section has an organ called the core that shines bright red. If you go to a beach toward the end of summer, the glowing cores of these POKéMON look like the stars in the sky.

STARYU apparently communicates with the stars in the night sky by flashing the red core at the center of its body. If parts of its body are torn, this POKéMON simply regenerates the missing pieces and limbs.
Fish Pokémon nibble at it, but Staryu isn’t bothered. Its body regenerates quickly, even if part of it is completely torn off.
Even if its body is torn, it can regenerate as long as the glowing central core re­ mains intact.
Even if its body is torn, it can regenerate as long as the glowing central core remains intact.
Large numbers of these Pokémon make their home at the seaside. At night, a strange red glow radiates from the center of their bodies.
If you visit a beach at the end of summer, you’ll be able to see groups of Staryu lighting up in a steady rhythm.
No number of injuries can bother Staryu. Its amazing regenerative powers return it to its previous state in half a day!
In many places, there are folktales of stardust falling into the ocean and becoming Staryu.
If its body is torn, it can grow back if the red core remains. The core flashes at midnight.
As long as its red core remains, it can regenerate its body instantly, even if it’s torn apart.
Even if its body is torn, it can regenerate as long as the glowing central core remains intact.
It appears in large numbers by seashores. At night, its central core flashes with a red light.
As long as the center section is unharmed, it can grow back fully even if it is chopped to bits.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Staryu apparently communicates with the stars in the night sky by flashing the red core at the center of its body. If parts of its body are torn, this Pokémon simply regenerates the missing pieces and limbs.
